b'
Hi, and welcome back to Line Sheet. Happy birthday to LVMH C.E.O. Bernard Arnault, the patron saint of this private email, who turned 76 this week. Today is Sarah Shapiro day in these parts. (Please email her at SShapiro@puck.news with comments, questions, and complaints.) She\xe2\x80\x99s here with thoughts on the future of Athleta, plus a whole lotta shopping intel\xe2\x80\x94from T.J. Maxx all the way to Galeries Lafayette.

Mar 07, 2025

Lauren Sherman

Hi, and welcome back to Line Sheet. Happy birthday to LVMH C.E.O. Bernard Arnault , the patron saint of this private email, who turned 76 this week.

Today is Sarah Shapiro day in these parts. (Please email her at SShapiro@puck.news with comments, questions, and complaints.) She\xe2\x80\x99s here with thoughts on the future of Athleta, plus a whole lotta shopping intel\xe2\x80\x94from T.J. Maxx all the way to Galeries Lafayette. (Both great shopping experiences, I.M.O.) Up top, in honor of Mr. Arnault\xe2\x80\x99s big day, I\xe2\x80\x99ve got some stories from Paris Fashion Week that all somehow lead back to LVMH, for better or worse.

For those of you with the Shoppies : As it is with every Paris Fashion Week, my eyes are bigger than my stomach when it comes to RSVPing for parties and events that I can\xe2\x80\x99t physically make it to. On Thursday, I missed Sophie Buhai \xe2\x80\x99s exhibition opening at Galerie Anne-Sophie Duval, and Herm\xc3\xa8s creative director of beauty Gregoris Pyrpylis \xe2\x80\x99s debut of his latest lip product, Rouge Brilliant Silky (I\xe2\x80\x99m a real lipstick person), and the launch event for the A Magazine Curated By 25th anniversary exhibition. Hopefully I make it to the opening of Bode\xe2\x80\x99s new store tonight.

Of course, one of the great things about Paris is running into people you know on the street, no formal mix-and-mingle necessary. The other day, after leaving Dover Street Market, I noticed a handsome man walking his shaggy dog. It was none other than Jan-Hendrik Schlottmann , the longtime business partner and husband of designer Derek Lam , maker of great pants. Jan and Derek moved to Paris a few years ago and now work on a line called C\xc3\xa2llas Milano. They asked me if they could send me a pair of Derek\xe2\x80\x99s pants. I said no, because I don\xe2\x80\x99t accept gifts, but I can\xe2\x80\x99t help but recommend the Tonne high-rise flared trouser to you anyway. The Jules, with a lower waist , is the other one you need. This silhouette is Derek\xe2\x80\x99s gift to the world.


Mentioned in this issue : Ernie Herrman , T.J. Maxx, Nike, Crocs, Glossier, LoveShackFancy, The North Face, Cecilie Bahnsen , LVMH, Gucci, Sarah Burton , Hubert de Givenchy , Antoine Arnault , Off-White, Ib Kamara , Virgil Abloh , Dior, Delphine Arnault , Daniel Roseberry , Rick Owens, Athleta, Beyonc\xc3\xa9 , Levi\xe2\x80\x99s, The Row, Phoebe Philo, Jane and Ron Herman , drawstring jeans, and many more\xe2\x80\xa6

A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR

Quietly powering the best in luxury. Learn more.

A Bunch of Things You Should Know\xe2\x80\xa6

  • The Paris Fashion Week that was, LVMH edition : On Friday, I had lunch with a friend who was coming straight from the Givenchy show, which took place in the LVMH-owned brand\xe2\x80\x99s offices in the 8th arrondissement. She distilled her review to one thought: the collection was good and distinctive enough to make you instantly forget the name Matthew M. Williams . Remember him? We wish Matthew all the luck in the world, of course\xe2\x80\x94 Riccardo Tisci , too\xe2\x80\x94but it\xe2\x80\x99s Sarah Burton \xe2\x80\x99s time now, and this era of her career is not about preserving the legacy of her predecessors (including longtime collaborator Alexander McQueen ). Nope, this was all about Burton, and what she thinks should define the house of Hubert de Givenchy : curved shoulders, big bows, clean tailoring, and cocktail dresses for hot women who love wearing black and trendy things. LVMH needs more brands that lead with the clothes, and this was that.

Photo: Courtesy of Givenchy

  • Stella McCartney, minority-owned by LVMH until very recently, used to be about the clothes. I\xe2\x80\x99m not quite sure what her show on the sixth floor of an office building in the northwestern suburbs was about, really. Preshow, there were performers in Working Girl \xe2\x80\x93style grey suiting flitting around; mid-show, there were dancers who sprang up from the audience to swing around strategically placed stripper poles (yes); and then there was the finale, choreographed to \xe2\x80\x9cTemporary Secretary,\xe2\x80\x9d her father\xe2\x80\x99s 1980 electropop single. (Weirdly, it became a cult classic.) If that all sounds bizarre and slightly off-color, it was.

    And yet, there\xe2\x80\x99s a lot of good will for McCartney . LVMH\xe2\x80\x99s Antoine Arnault came out to support her\xe2\x80\x94his wife, model Natalia Vodianova , opened the show\xe2\x80\x94and McCartney\xe2\x80\x99s original business partners in the venture, Tom Ford and Domenico De Sole , were there, too. (De Sole\xe2\x80\x99s face when the pole dancing started\xe2\x80\x94void of judgment and enjoyment\xe2\x80\x94offered a glimpse into what makes him both a shrewd and compassionate negotiator.) Anyway, I don\xe2\x80\x99t know where McCartney goes from here, but I hope that however she sets up her business going forward, the focus is back on clothes that send positive vibes.

    Sadly, I missed Off-White, another brand that was sold off by LVMH in recent months. Like most people, I assumed that the label\xe2\x80\x99s new owner, licensing firm Bluestar Alliance, would nix creative director Ib Kamara \xe2\x80\x99s runway collection straight away, since brand management firms are in the business of printing, not spending, money. But I was glad to hear from pretty much everyone that Kamara, who still styles editorials for the likes of Vogue and remains editor-in-chief of indie pub Dazed , did a great job making Off-White his own. Arguably, Off-White was never really about the runway, even when the late Virgil Abloh was still designing it, but it\xe2\x80\x99s nice to see that Bluestar wants to give it a go. I\xe2\x80\x99ve heard they\xe2\x80\x99re making a big investment in the brand on top of what they paid for it\xe2\x80\x94a hopeful signal for those who fear Abloh\xe2\x80\x99s legacy will be damaged by such an alliance (pun intended).

    Schiaparelli is not owned by LVMH, but Dior C.E.O. Delphine Arnault was at Daniel Roseberry \xe2\x80\x99s ready-to-wear show on Thursday night. Don\xe2\x80\x99t get all excited: There are plenty of ways to explain her appearance. Delphine is a patron of young designers\xe2\x80\x94she spearheaded the LVMH Prize, and has nurtured relationships with the likes of Jonathan Anderson and others\xe2\x80\x94but Schiaparelli owner Diego Della Valle is a longtime ally of her father, and a longtime member of the LVMH board. Anyway, it wasn\xe2\x80\x99t just Arnault fille who came out for Roseberry: The entire Saks Global team, including chairman Richard Baker and C.E.O. Marc Metrick , was there as they navigate this, uh, tricky time with vendors. Credit goes to Roseberry for commanding attention. While his couture shows are the thing, I thought this ready-to-wear collection\xe2\x80\x94a corseted pileup of black denim and gold belts\xe2\x80\x94was the strongest and most polished I\xe2\x80\x99ve seen to date. I also loved the country soundtrack, which launched with a rendition of John Denver \xe2\x80\x99s \xe2\x80\x9cTake Me Home, Country Roads.\xe2\x80\x9d Roseberry is always reminding us that he is an American in Paris.

    Although there are plenty of those these days. (Louis Vuitton womenswear designer Nicolas Ghesqui\xc3\xa8re is really the only French person currently leading one of the big LVMH houses.) Rick Owens is still indie, free from the shackles of the word \xe2\x80\x9cstrategic,\xe2\x80\x9d although every season I wonder whether he\xe2\x80\x99ll sell. I assume he could: There are few brands with such a loyal and consistent following\xe2\x80\x94not to mention a clear path of category expansion. (The world needs more Rick Owens bags; something you can\xe2\x80\x99t say for any other brands.) This season\xe2\x80\x99s show at the fog machine\xe2\x80\x93filled Palais de Tokyo was incredibly elegant, and dare I say commercial , set to Iggy Pop \xe2\x80\x99s \xe2\x80\x9cMass Production,\xe2\x80\x9d harkening back to the soundtrack for Owens\xe2\x80\x99 very first New York show. I especially loved Look 14, a rose-colored top made of what looked like slices of curved plastic and paired with a long sweater skirt in cream. Some models were carrying beauty cases from a sold-out collaboration with Rimowa, which, yes, is owned by LVMH. In Paris, there\xe2\x80\x99s no escaping it.
  • Sarah on the Athleta \xe2\x80\x9creset\xe2\x80\x9d : While Gap Inc.\xe2\x80\x99s latest earnings call was nearly all good news, it didn\xe2\x80\x99t escape notice that C.E.O. Richard Dickson used the dreaded term \xe2\x80\x9creset\xe2\x80\x9d more than once while talking about Athleta, the beleaguered, female-focused athletic wear brand. Indeed, there\xe2\x80\x99s an identity crisis brewing in Gap Inc.\xe2\x80\x99s athletic wear division: Dickson\xe2\x80\x99s team is leaning into Old Navy activewear for the whole family (the brand\xe2\x80\x99s StudioSmooth leggings are plastered across the homepage ), with a shockingly low price point that\xe2\x80\x99s catapulted it into fifth place in the activewear market. Athleta, meanwhile, occupies a female-only niche that\xe2\x80\x99s neither budget-friendly nor premium-cool, and its Gen Z/Millennial marketing meshes awkwardly with a product that\xe2\x80\x99s actually more Gen X/Boomer. All of which raises the question of whether Old Navy will ultimately muscle out its corporate sister.

    We\xe2\x80\x99ve seen Gap Inc. play with its portfolio composition before\xe2\x80\x94most recently, when it sold off Intermix in 2021. While Gap\xe2\x80\x99s leadership remains vaguely optimistic about Athleta, the brand does stand apart from other Gap Inc. businesses, as Intermix always did. For a time, it was based in laid-back, old-timey Petaluma before being dragged down to Gap headquarters at 2 Folsom. Meanwhile, leadership hasn\xe2\x80\x99t shared an actual strategy beyond the aforementioned \xe2\x80\x9creset,\xe2\x80\x9d which needs to happen soon if Athleta wants to keep pace with Lululemon, Alo Yoga, and yes, NikeSkims . \xe2\x80\x94Sarah Shapiro

And now, the main event\xe2\x80\xa6

The Week in Shopping: Foot Traffic, New Collabs & Drawstrings

News and notes on T.J. Maxx\xe2\x80\x99s offline flex, The North Face\xe2\x80\x99s Paris play, Savette\xe2\x80\x99s no-logo-but-you-know handbags, and the rise of drawstring jeans.

Sarah Shapiro

Stores are always looking for strategies to create foot traffic in this mobile shopping world. One tactic, of course, is to limit your online presence. T.J. Maxx, for instance, had the highest foot traffic in the eastern United States last quarter, in part because new merchandise arrives every week\xe2\x80\x94you have to actually walk into a store to see the latest. The \xe2\x80\x9cThrill of the Retail Hunt\xe2\x80\x9d strategy predates C.E.O. Ernie Herrman \xe2\x80\x94remember the \xe2\x80\x9cMaxxinista\xe2\x80\x9d campaign?\xe2\x80\x94but it\xe2\x80\x99s especially retro amid an era where everything is online. Then again, T.J. Maxx has over 1,300 U.S. stores\xe2\x80\x94not Walmart territory, but enough of a retail footprint to be pretty ubiquitous.

A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR

Turning influence into ROI. Learn more.

Herrman has also been employing another traditional engagement tactic: offering desirable brands across multiple price points. Obviously, countless top-tier brands drive revenue by selling older, discounted merchandise at chains like T.J. Maxx, or by creating lower-priced items just for those stores. In the Hamptons, for instance, eagle-eyed shoppers have even unearthed sweaters and gala pants from The Row. During my tour of a few T.J. Maxx stores in California, I found A.L.C., re/done denim, Nike, Crocs, Glossier, and LoveShackFancy. Plus, as Junior departments shrink or disappear, T.J. Maxx offers options for those tricky tween years, which also engenders store loyalty as buyers grow up. As one analyst pointed out to me, given their strong earnings in the last fiscal quarter, T.J. Maxx wins no matter what the economy seems to be doing.

The Collab Report

Meanwhile, I was fascinated to see North Face show up on Cecilie Bahnsen \xe2\x80\x99s runway during Paris Fashion Week, with the results now available at the local Dover Street Market. It\xe2\x80\x99s a savvy if unexpected pairing: a functional-leaning, albeit fashionable, outdoor performance brand intertwined with a super feminine and romantic counterpart. Bahnsen launched her namesake line in 2015, and it became a finalist for the LVMH Prize two years later. The North Face, meanwhile, has been orchestrating collaborations with the likes of Gucci and Supreme for years, giving the anchor brand of parentco VF Corp a lift when the rest of the portfolio was dragging.

Buckle Up

On TikTok, Gen Z-Alphas seem to have rediscovered the timeworn teenage tradition of using a shoelace as a makeshift belt to tighten loose-fitting pants\xe2\x80\x94and Hollister, like many brands before them, decided to crib the \xe2\x80\x9cinnovation\xe2\x80\x9d for these super baggy jeans. I doubt the more nuanced takes from brands like Citizen of Humanity , Mother , and La Ligne are responding to the TikTok trend, but the idea that a pair of jeans can be adjusted on the fly, even slightly, can make the difference between keeping or returning the item. (Even Beyonc\xc3\xa9 knows how hard it can be to get the right fit.)

When I spoke to La Ligne C.E.O. Molly Howard , she told me the drawstring denim was inspired by their popular, comfort-is-king Colby pants. Jane Herman , who writes the Substack Jane on Jeans and whose father is denim trend-spotter and shop owner Ron Herman , also reminded me that Levi\xe2\x80\x99s first pair of jeans , the waist overalls, actually had a back buckle to adjust fit. They\xe2\x80\x99re still using a similar design with Levi\xe2\x80\x99s Cinch Jeans .

Herman also told me she recently picked up Rachel Comey \xe2\x80\x99s Elkin Pants with a back buckle, and that people stop her in the street when she wears Frame\xe2\x80\x99s drawstring jeans . The Buckle denim from Still Here, with two side-adjustable buckles, reminds me of how kids\xe2\x80\x99 jeans often have that inside, adjustable button waistband with elastic. Time really is a flat circle.

Shop Du Jour

Savette handbags have managed to break into a market\xe2\x80\x94Europe, and specifically, Europe\xe2\x80\x99s snobby fashion people\xe2\x80\x94that\xe2\x80\x99s usually resistant to a particular breed of indie, relatively well-priced brands. Designer and founder Amy Zurek designed handbags for The Row, Khaite, and even the resurging Coach. Her meticulously crafted accessories are made from sumptuous materials, and the brand offers plenty of clutch and handbag styles that pivot nicely from their slouchier counterparts. These include the timeless Symmetry Pouchette , which is now showing up in the hands of style arbiters (the clasp and styling tell you it\xe2\x80\x99s Savette: no logo needed). Savette has also opened some meaningful doors, and can now be found on the floors of Le Bon March\xc3\xa9, Luisaviaroma, 10 Corso Como, and Liberty.

Meanwhile, The Row and Phoebe Philo\xe2\x80\x99s arrival at Galeries Lafayette marks a strategic evolution for both labels as they balance calculated scarcity with measured accessibility. Beyond expanding their Paris footprint (The Row opened a flagship store there last fall), the move signals their confidence in the French and European luxury market, while courting the customer who values in-person shopping and tactile connection to the garments. They\xe2\x80\x99re engaged in a careful distribution dance, one that expands reach while maintaining an aura of exclusivity.

The Week in Feedback: \xe2\x80\x9cWho Is Going to Gucci?\xe2\x80\x9d

\xe2\x80\x9c Demna to Gucci! Pieter Mulier to Balenciaga! No, wait, Pieter to Gucci!\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 Italian people

\xe2\x80\x9cIt\xe2\x80\x99s not Maria Grazia .\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94An executive

\xe2\x80\x9cDid you ever think that Tom Ford might be going back to Gucci?\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 A client

\xe2\x80\x9cRemember that Gucci has never hired a known name.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 A C.E.O.

\xe2\x80\x9cForget about Dario [ Vitale ] for a minute, what about [former Prada design director] Fabio Zambernardi ?\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 A pretty smart person

\xe2\x80\x9cThey should pay Hedi [ Slimane ] whatever it costs. It will be worth every penny.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 An enigma who knows everything

See you at Herm\xc3\xa8s tomorrow,
Lauren

P.S. : We are using affiliate links because we are a business. We may make a couple bucks off them.

Puck founding partner Matt Belloni takes you inside the business of Hollywood, using exclusive reporting and insight to explain the backstories on everything from Marvel movies to the streaming wars.

An essential, insider-friendly Hollywood tip sheet from Matthew Belloni, who spent 14 years in the trenches at The Hollywood Reporter and five before that practicing entertainment law. What I\xe2\x80\x99m Hearing also features veteran Hollywood journalist Kim Masters, as well as a special companion email from Eriq Gardner, focused on entertainment law, and weekly box office analysis from Scott Mendelson.

Need help? Review our FAQ page or contact us for assistance. For brand partnerships, email ads@puck.news .

You received this email because you signed up to receive emails from Puck, or as part of your Puck account associated with puck@khamel.com. To stop receiving this newsletter and/or manage all your email preferences, click here .

Puck is published by Heat Media LLC.\xc2\xa0107\xc2\xa0Greenwich\xc2\xa0St, New York, NY 10006
